metadata.rationale: L’article s’appuie sur des faits réels distincts — une commande AerCap et une commande de VSR700 — mais les fusionne en un récit exagéré de relance industrielle immédiate et déjà visible sur les sites Airbus. Les images renforcent abusivement cette lecture en suggérant, par édition et mise en scène positive, qu’elles documentent directement cette séquence précise alors qu’elles n’en apportent pas la preuve.

I will proceed to formally detect the image content to extract specific entity mentions such as the airline, aircraft model, and text on the signage, ensuring my analysis of the "AerCap" order and "A320neo" models is precise before providing the final verdict.
best_trajectory.observation:
- **No obvious signs of AI synthesis:** The aircraft, runway environment, background planes, and heat haze/atmospheric softness look photographically consistent. Edges (landing gear, engine nacelle, tail) are clean without the “melted” artifacts often seen in generated images.  
- **Lighting and shadows are coherent:** The plane’s shading and the ground tones match a single light direction; no mismatched highlights or unnatural shadow discontinuities.  
- **Text and logos look plausible:** The AerCap sign in the foreground has crisp, readable bilingual text with consistent typography and perspective; the A320neo-style “neo” and “AIRBUS A320” markings on the fuselage look aligned with the aircraft curvature rather than pasted flat.  
- **Depth/perspective consistency:** Foreground sign, midground grass/taxiway, and the aircraft scale/position feel consistent; no obvious cut-and-paste seams around the plane.  
- **Minor note:** The image appears to be a composite-style layout only in the sense that it’s a single framed shot with a prominent foreground sign; nothing indicates the sign or aircraft was digitally inserted.

**Overall:** The image appears authentic with no clear visual evidence of manipulation or synthetic generation.

- **Airbus “neo” branding (“neo” + “unbeatable fuel efficiency”)** — High certainty (large “neo” and slogan visible).
- **Airbus logo/name (“AIRBUS”)** — High certainty (visible near the front of the aircraft).
- **Aercap (logo + text “AERCAP”)** — High certainty (clearly readable on the foreground sign).
- **“Préparation livraison / Delivery preparation” sign (delivery-prep area)** — High certainty (bilingual text readable).
- **Airport runway/taxiway environment (airfield, grass verge, paved strip, light poles)** — Medium–high certainty (typical airport ground infrastructure visible).
- **Other aircraft in the background (unidentified operators/models)** — Medium certainty (planes visible but markings not clear enough to identify).
